schools.summary: Gill: they were convened at Mizpeh before the war; they sware not to give a daughter to Benjamin to wife.

themes: There shall not any of us give his daughter unto Benjamin to wife
the danger of rash oaths sworn in passion, breeding perplexity
be not rash with thy mouth... to utter any thing before God (Eccl. 5:2)

schools.summary: Gill: it was to Shiloh, where the tabernacle and ark were, not Bethel; they abode there till even and wept sore.

themes: the people came to the house of God, and abode there till even before God, and lifted up their voices, and wept sore
bringing their sorrows to God and mourning before his face
they shall come with weeping, and with supplications will I lead them (Jer. 31:9)

schools.summary: Gill: they addressed Jehovah, the covenant God of all the twelve tribes; asked why one tribe was lacking.

themes: O LORD God of Israel, why is this come to pass in Israel, that there should be to day one tribe lacking in Israel?
mourning the breaches among the brethren, yet searching one's own heart for the cause
let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the LORD (Lam. 3:40)

schools.summary: Gill: the day after their fasting and prayer they rose early; built an altar and offered burnt and peace offerings.

themes: the people rose early, and built there an altar, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings
seeking God in distress through sacrifice, for mercy and communion
having made peace through the blood of his cross (Col. 1:20)

schools.summary: Gill: this was asked to bring to justice those who came not up, by their oath.
Ellicott: the verse touches the great oath they had made.

themes: Who is there among all the tribes of Israel that came not up with the congregation unto the LORD? For they had made a great oath
the entangling power of rash vows driving men from one strait to another
it is a snare... after vows to make enquiry (Prov. 20:25)

schools.summary: Gill: not that the war was unjust, but they grieved for the severity, especially toward the innocent; one tribe cut off this day.

themes: the children of Israel repented them for Benjamin their brother, and said, There is one tribe cut off from Israel this day
remembering mercy and grieving at the wounds of the brethren even after just severity
if a man be overtaken in a fault... restore such an one in the spirit of meekness (Gal. 6:1)

schools.summary: Gill: it seems they knew of the six hundred hid in the rock Rimmon.
Clarke: the perplexity, how shall we do for wives?

themes: How shall we do for wives for them that remain, seeing we have sworn by the LORD that we will not give them of our daughters to wives?
the knots a hasty vow ties, driving men to shifts to escape their own words
whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles (Prov. 21:23)

schools.summary: Gill: this was asked to find a way for wives as well as to do justice; none came from Jabesh-gilead.

themes: there came none to the camp from Jabeshgilead to the assembly
men joining a show of righteous judgment to their own ends and expediency
not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together (Heb. 10:25)

schools.summary: Gill: they numbered to know who came and who did not, whether Jabesh-gilead were there; none were found.

themes: the people were numbered, and, behold, there were none of the inhabitants of Jabeshgilead there
exactness in keeping an oath no virtue when its execution leads to evil
to obey is better than sacrifice (1 Sam. 15:22)

schools.summary: Gill: they sent twelve thousand of the valiantest; commanded them to smite the inhabitants with the sword.

themes: Go and smite the inhabitants of Jabeshgilead with the edge of the sword
the entanglement of an unwise vow driving men into compounded evil
they proceed from evil to evil, and they know not me, saith the LORD (Jer. 9:3)

schools.summary: Gill: this was the thing given them in charge to execute.
Keil & Delitzsch: the command to utterly destroy every male and every woman that had known man.

themes: Ye shall utterly destroy every male, and every woman that hath lain by man
the grievous cost of vows rashly sworn, exacting blood and sorrow unforeseen
better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than that thou shouldest vow and not pay (Eccl. 5:5)

schools.summary: Gill: they were damsels that were virgins, judged by their age; brought them to the camp to Shiloh in Canaan.

themes: they found among the inhabitants of Jabeshgilead four hundred young virgins... and they brought them unto the camp to Shiloh
a way opened to preserve Benjamin even amid severe judgment
in wrath remember mercy (Hab. 3:2)

schools.summary: Gill: they sent messengers to call them and desire them to come.
Clarke: they called peaceably unto them.

themes: sent some to speak to the children of Benjamin that were in the rock Rimmon, and to call peaceably unto them
seeking peace and the restoring of the estranged brethren after chastisement
the work of righteousness shall be peace (Isa. 32:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the six hundred returned, trusting the assurances of peace; the women sufficed them not, four hundred for six hundred.

themes: they gave them wives which they had saved alive of the women of Jabeshgilead: and yet so they sufficed them not
the remedies of man's devising incomplete, leaving a lack that drives to further shifts
the way of transgressors is hard (Prov. 13:15)

schools.summary: Gill: they repented they had destroyed all the women and saved no more of Jabesh-gilead; the LORD had made a breach in the tribes.

themes: the people repented them for Benjamin, because that the LORD had made a breach in the tribes of Israel
owning the LORD's hand in the chastisement for the nation's sin
I will bear the indignation of the LORD, because I have sinned against him (Mic. 7:9)

schools.summary: Gill: there were not wives enough, so they consulted again for another expedient.

themes: How shall we do for wives for them that remain, seeing the women are destroyed out of Benjamin?
the troubles bred by sin and rash vows not soon ended, but multiplying
fools make a mock at sin (Prov. 14:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the escaped were the six hundred; four hundred had wives, two hundred wanted; a tribe be not destroyed.

themes: There must be an inheritance for them that be escaped of Benjamin, that a tribe be not destroyed out of Israel
God's providence preserving every tribe of the covenant people
the LORD will not forsake his people for his great name's sake (1 Sam. 12:22)

schools.summary: Gill: their case was necessitous and desperate, yet they would not give their daughters.
Keil & Delitzsch: the oath, cursed be he that giveth a wife to Benjamin.

themes: we may not give them wives of our daughters: for the children of Israel have sworn... Cursed be he that giveth a wife to Benjamin
a vow unwisely made still binding, driving men to strange shifts
swear not at all... but let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay (Matt. 5:34, 37)

schools.summary: Gill: the elders debated ways to assist Benjamin and preserve the tribe.
Keil & Delitzsch: there was a feast of the LORD in Shiloh yearly.

themes: Behold, there is a feast of the LORD in Shiloh yearly
men bending even sacred occasions to their own contrivances
keep the feast... with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth (1 Cor. 5:8)

schools.summary: Gill: they ordered the two hundred men that wanted wives, speaking with authority as elders; to lie in wait in the vineyards.

themes: Therefore they commanded the children of Benjamin, saying, Go and lie in wait in the vineyards
a rash vow leading even leaders into doubtful and disorderly devices
he that walketh uprightly walketh surely (Prov. 10:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the daughters of Shiloh danced by themselves at the festival, so might more easily be taken; each man to catch his wife.

themes: if the daughters of Shiloh come out to dance in dances, then come ye out of the vineyards, and catch you every man his wife
where right rule is wanting, even the joys of God's feasts are marred by men's devices
let all things be done decently and in order (1 Cor. 14:40)

schools.summary: Gill: the fathers might come to complain of the rape of their daughters; they would say, ye did not give them, that ye should be guilty.

debates: Was the oath truly kept, or only evaded?

The witnesses note the elders' reasoning keeps the letter (the fathers did not 'give' their daughters; the men took them) while plainly evading the spirit of the vow. It is recorded as the shift of a people entangled by a rash oath, not commended as honest dealing; God regards the heart, not the loophole (Matt. 23:23).
themes: we will say unto them, Be favourable unto them for our sakes... ye did not give unto them at this time, that ye should be guilty
keeping the letter of a vow while evading its spirit; God looks on the truth within
ye... have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith (Matt. 23:23)

schools.summary: Gill: they laid wait in the vineyards and seized the daughters as they came to dance.
Keil & Delitzsch: them repairing the cities and dwelling in them.

themes: the children of Benjamin... took them wives... and they... repaired the cities, and dwelt in them
the mercy that preserved the tribe and replanted them in their inheritance
I will build them, and not pull them down (Jer. 24:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the war being ended and things settled as well as circumstances would admit; every man went to his tribe, family, and inheritance.

themes: the children of Israel departed thence at that time, every man to his tribe and to his family... to his inheritance
God granting a measure of rest after the storms of judgment
he maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth (Ps. 46:9)

schools.summary: Gill: there was no supreme magistrate, Joshua being dead and no judge yet risen for these events.
Keil & Delitzsch: every man did that which was right in his own eyes.

themes: In those days there was no king in Israel: every man did that which was right in his own eyes
where God's rule is cast off, every man becomes his own law and evil follows; the need of the King
of the increase of his government and peace there shall be no end... upon the throne of David (Isa. 9:7)
